please translate the below to English:
目前理论上关于 Zcs(3985) 的研究非常热烈，给出了各种可能的内部结构的解释，比如紧致型四夸克态、强子分子态、夸克对-反夸克对态，等等。此外欧洲核子中心大型强子对撞机上的LHCb实验在2021年3月在底介子到 ϕ 介子、J/ψ粲偶素和带电 K 介子的衰变过程中发现了衰变到粲偶素 J/ψ 和带电K介子的两个新的奇特四夸克态Zcs(4000) 和 Zcs(4220) ，质 量 分 别 为 4.003 GeV 和4.216 GeV，衰变宽度分别为 131 MeV 和 233 MeV。这进一步了确认了含奇异夸克的隐粲四夸克态 Zcs的存在。

Currently, there is intense theoretical research on the Zcs(3985), which provides various possible explanations for its internal structure, such as compact tetraquark states, hadronic molecular states, quark-antiquark pair states, and so on. Additionally, in March 2021, the LHCb experiment at the European Organization for Nuclear Research (CERN) made significant discoveries in the decay processes of bottom mesons to phi mesons, J/psi charmonium, and charged K mesons. Two new exotic tetraquark states, Zcs(4000) and Zcs(4220), were observed decaying into J/psi charmonium and charged K mesons. These states have masses of 4.003 GeV and 4.216 GeV, and decay widths of 131 MeV and 233 MeV, respectively. This further confirms the existence of the hidden-charm tetraquark state Zcs containing strange quarks.

However, LHCb and BES recently planning to release the new results of pentaquarks observation related to $\bar{D}\Lambda_c$ molecule, where the $\Lambda_c$ belongs to the same asymmetric representation $\bar{3}_A$ as $\Xi_c$ within $Pcs(4338/4459)$ molecules.

However, LHCb and BES have recently announced their plans to release new results regarding the observation of pentaquarks associated with the $\bar{D}\Lambda_c$ molecule. It is noteworthy that the $\Lambda_c$ and $\Xi_c$ both belong to the same asymmetric representation, denoted as $\bar{3}_A$, within the $Pcs(4338/4459)$ molecules.
the a belongs to the same asymmetric representation o as b
The entity "a" belongs to the same asymmetric representation as "b".
the Alice belongs to the same asymmetric representation of O as Bob
Alice belongs to the same asymmetric representation as Bob within the entity "O".
thus provide us the opportunity to estimate the SU(3) flavor break effect in heavy hadron molecules description of exotics.
Therefore, this provides us with the opportunity to estimate the effects of SU(3) flavor breaking in the description of exotic heavy hadron molecules.

The SU(3) flavor breaking effect refers to the deviation from strict SU(3) symmetry in systems involving quarks with different flavors. In particle physics, flavor is a quantum number that distinguishes different types of quarks. The SU(3) flavor symmetry assumes that all quark flavors (up, down, strange, charm, top, and bottom) are equivalent.

However, in reality, quarks of different flavors have slightly different masses, leading to flavor breaking. This breaking of SU(3) symmetry introduces distinctions and variations in the properties and interactions of particles containing different flavors of quarks.

The effects of SU(3) flavor breaking are particularly relevant when studying exotic hadrons, such as tetraquarks or pentaquarks, where a combination of quarks with different flavors forms an unconventional structure. Accounting for SU(3) flavor breaking is crucial to accurately describe and understand the properties and dynamics of these exotic particles.

please polish the sentence below:
Einstein has said that "the most incomprehensible thing about the world is that it is comprehensible" and EFT could give us a hint of the answer to this puzzle.

Einstein once remarked that "the most incomprehensible thing about the world is that it is comprehensible." In this regard, the application of Effective Field Theory (EFT) might provide us with a valuable clue towards unraveling this enigma.
